steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The phrase “nearly the same for both” directly shows that the program's performance on Washington, DC images matched its performance on Boston images.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
“Similar to” directly expresses the equivalence indicated by “nearly the same” — the program's performance on DC was comparable to its performance on Boston.

The other three choices either contradict this equivalence (unique from) or make no logical sense in comparing performance metrics (uncertain about, supportive toward).

That confirms B is correct.
